description: Protected-baseline oracle for the machine tier (data/xps/elements-machine.json). Maps transition id -> the full record as currently shipped. The guard test_existing_machine_records_byte_unchanged_vs_snapshot asserts every record listed here is still present and structurally identical in the live machine file, catching silent mutation of already-shipped NIST reference energies. DURABILITY: future coverage expansions add NEW ids that are simply absent here, so they neither break nor are protected by this guard until the baseline is intentionally extended. Regenerate ONLY as a deliberate act when you mean to extend the protected baseline (e.g. after a reviewed coverage expansion) -- never to silence a failure, since a diff here means an existing record changed. Regenerate with: python -c "import json;d=json.load(open('data/xps/elements-machine.json'));r={t['id']:t for el in d['elements'] for f in el['families'] for t in f['transitions']};o=json.load(open('tests/fixtures/machine_records_snapshot.json'));o['records']=dict(sorted(r.items()));json.dump(o,open('tests/fixtures/machine_records_snapshot.json','w'),indent=2,ensure_ascii=False)"
